creator: matt@megaweapon.zigg.com
text:
OK.  I didn't realize, since I only use socks5 as a proxy server for my
poorly written PC clients :)

The PR can be closed.

On Sat, 21 Nov 1998, Andre Albsmeier wrote:

> On Fri, 20-Nov-1998 at 18:48:57 -0800, matt@zigg.com wrote:
> > 
> > >Number:         8777
> > >Category:       ports
> > >Synopsis:       ports/socks5 out of date
> > >Confidential:   no
> > >Severity:       non-critical
> > >Priority:       medium
> > >Responsible:    freebsd-ports
> > >State:          open
> > >Quarter:

> > FreeBSD megaweapon.zigg.com 2.2.7-STABLE FreeBSD 2.2.7-STABLE #0: Mon Nov  2 18:34:58 EST 1998     matt@megaweapon.zigg.com:/usr/src/sys/compile/MEGAWEAPON  i386
> > >Description:
> > net/socks5 is out of date.  The version of socks5 currently distributed at http://www.socks.nec.com/ is 1.0r8, not 1.0r7.
> > >How-To-Repeat:
> > 
> > >Fix:
> > Remove patch-ab and patch-ac (they seem to be integrated in 1.0r7), update md5 and Makefile.
> > >Audit-Trail:
> > >Unformatted:
> > 
> > To Unsubscribe: send mail to majordomo@FreeBSD.org
> > with "unsubscribe freebsd-ports" in the body of the message
> 
> Let's wait a little bit. v8's runsocks is broken on FreeBSD. When using
> v7's lib/rld.c file it works. I have already sent a message about
> that to the socks5 list.
> 
> 	-Andre
>
